cases.assessmentExcerpt: Task refusal and Physical Aggression are documented. Premack Principle and Differential Reinforcement of Alternative Behavior (DRA) are available.
paragraphs: At the dining table, the RBT presented a worksheet and the client manifested Task refusal by pushing the materials away. The RBT implemented Premack Principle. Following this intervention, the RBT presented one task step and reinforced completion. The RBT implemented the replacement program "Compliance Training" through single-step directions; approximately 20% of discrete trials met criterion.

In the living room, the client manifested Physical Aggression by hitting the RBT's arm after access to a ball was delayed. The RBT implemented Differential Reinforcement of Alternative Behavior (DRA). Following this intervention, the RBT reinforced keeping hands to self. The RBT implemented the replacement program "Accept “no” as a response"; approximately 10% of discrete trials met criterion.

cases.assessmentExcerpt: Task refusal and Physical Aggression are documented.
paragraphs: The RBT presented a chore list and the client manifested Task refusal by turning away from the table. The RBT implemented Escape independent response delivery. Following this intervention, the RBT re-presented smaller task steps. The RBT implemented "Schedule of activities" by reviewing current and next tasks; criterion was met on approximately 10% of discrete trials.

The client manifested Physical Aggression by pushing the RBT's forearm when a snack was unavailable. The RBT implemented Response blocking. Following this intervention, the RBT prevented further contact. The RBT implemented Differential Reinforcement of Alternative Behavior (DRA). Following this intervention, the RBT reinforced an appropriate choice. The RBT implemented "Accepting alternatives and making choices"; criterion was met on approximately 10% of discrete trials.

The RBT presented a folding task and the client manifested Task refusal by pushing clothing back into a bin. The RBT implemented Premack Principle. Following this intervention, the RBT reinforced completed steps. The RBT implemented "Schedule of activities" before transitions; criterion was met on approximately 10% of discrete trials.

cases.assessmentExcerpt: Elopement and Property destruction are documented.

hourlyAssignments.programName: Request permission to leave the unsupervised area

paragraphs: The client manifested Elopement by leaving the work area after a worksheet was presented. The RBT implemented Response blocking. Following this intervention, the RBT stopped further movement. The RBT implemented Escape independent response delivery. Following this intervention, the RBT prompted an appropriate request. The RBT implemented "Request permission to leave the unsupervised area"; criterion was met on approximately 30% of discrete trials.

The client manifested Property destruction by tearing a reading page during a written task. The RBT implemented Differential Reinforcement of Alternative Behavior (DRA). Following this intervention, the RBT reinforced keeping materials intact. The RBT implemented "Remaining seated, completing the task"; approximately 30% of discrete trials met criterion.

cases.assessmentExcerpt: Property destruction, Self-injury behavior (SIB), and Physical Aggression are documented.

hourlyAssignments.programName: Express and accept opinion, agreement and disagreement

paragraphs: The client manifested Property destruction by tearing a worksheet during table work. The RBT implemented Response blocking. Following this intervention, the RBT prevented further tearing. The RBT implemented Escape independent response delivery. Following this intervention, the RBT presented shorter task steps. The RBT implemented "Compliance Training" through short directions; criterion was met on approximately 20% of discrete trials.

The client manifested Self-injury behavior (SIB) by striking his face during a card game. The RBT implemented Response blocking. Following this intervention, the RBT prevented further contact. The RBT implemented Differential Reinforcement of Alternative Behavior (DRA). Following this intervention, the RBT reinforced hands remaining away from the face. The RBT implemented "Express and accept opinion, agreement and disagreement" through modeled statements; criterion was met on approximately 20% of discrete trials.

The client manifested Physical Aggression by attempting to hit during a cleanup direction. The RBT implemented Response blocking. Following this intervention, the RBT prevented further contact. The RBT implemented Premack Principle. Following this intervention, the RBT reinforced completed cleanup steps. The RBT implemented "Compliance Training" across household directions; criterion was met on approximately 20% of discrete trials.

The client manifested Property destruction by tearing notebook materials during written work. The RBT implemented Differential Reinforcement of Alternative Behavior (DRA). Following this intervention, the RBT reinforced keeping materials intact. The RBT implemented "Time on Task" through short work intervals; criterion was met on approximately 20% of discrete trials.

cases.assessmentExcerpt: Task Refusal includes tossing materials aside and withdrawing from a presented task. The assessment contains broad teaching terminology that does not expand the approved profile intervention list.

profileBehaviorTargets.name: Task Refusal
profileBehaviorTargets.topography: tossing task materials aside and withdrawing from the activity

paragraphs: During a coloring activity, the client manifested Task Refusal by tossing the marker aside and withdrawing from the activity after the cap did not open. The RBT implemented Environmental Manipulation. Following this intervention, the RBT arranged accessible materials. The RBT implemented Prompting. Following this intervention, the RBT delivered a verbal cue for requesting assistance. The RBT implemented offer choices. Following this intervention, the RBT presented gesture, word, and picture communication options. The RBT implemented the replacement program "Request help" by reinforcing each help-seeking response; criterion was met on approximately 10% of discrete trials.

cases.assessmentExcerpt: The client receives home-based services. Registered behavior descriptions and approved interventions are supplied by the profile.

profileBehaviorTargets.name: Repetitive Behavior
profileBehaviorTargets.topography: continuous hand movements up and down or sideways, or tapping furniture with hands more than three times

profileBehaviorTargets.name: Tantrum
profileBehaviorTargets.topography: crying, yelling, or dropping to the floor during a presented demand

profileBehaviorTargets.name: Task Refusal
profileBehaviorTargets.topography: pushing task materials away or turning the body away from the presented task

profileBehaviorTargets.name: Physical Aggression
profileBehaviorTargets.topography: contacting another person's body with an open hand

paragraphs: At the living room table, the client manifested Repetitive Behavior through continuous hand movements up and down. The RBT implemented Visual Supports. Following this intervention, the RBT used picture cards to represent each activity. The RBT implemented the replacement program "Schedule of activities"; criterion was met on approximately 10% of discrete trials.

During a matching activity, the client manifested Tantrum by crying during the presented demand. The RBT implemented Prompting. Following this intervention, the RBT delivered a concise verbal direction. The RBT implemented the replacement program "Request for Break"; criterion was met on approximately 10% of discrete trials.

During table work, the client manifested Task Refusal by pushing task materials away. The RBT implemented Premack Principle. Following this intervention, the RBT presented one brief response requirement before documented reinforcement. The RBT implemented the replacement program "Compliance Training"; criterion was met on approximately 10% of discrete trials.

During block play, the client manifested Physical Aggression by contacting the RBT's arm with an open hand. The RBT implemented Response blocking. Following this intervention, the RBT prevented additional contact. The RBT implemented the replacement program "Accept alternatives"; criterion was met on approximately 10% of discrete trials.
